title: psyllium arborescens subsp. maderensis
intro: Psyllium arborescens subsp. maderensis, also known as the Madeiran psyllium, is a plant species in the Plantaginaceae family that is native to the island of Madeira in the Atlantic Ocean. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, due to its rich content of mucilage, a thick and gel-like substance. The health benefits of Psyllium arborescens subsp. maderensis include promoting healthy gut bacteria, reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, and supporting healthy blood sugar levels. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues and other health concerns.
